annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required for default criteria

title: 1.1: Creates a standalone function named getPanelDataSummary that accepts DataFrames and returns a PanelDataSummary

annotations.description: A new exported function named getPanelDataSummary exists at packages/grafana-data/src/panel/suggestions/getPanelDataSummary.ts. It accepts an array of DataFrame objects (with a default of empty array) and returns a PanelDataSummary, callable without instantiating VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 85-128 show the new function: export function getPanelDataSummary(frames: DataFrame[] = []): PanelDataSummary.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Can you refactor the data summary into a standalone utility that can be used independently of the builder? The summary should be available as a simple function call that takes dataframes and returns the summary.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface specifies: Path: packages/grafana-data/src/panel/suggestions/getPanelDataSummary.ts, Name: getPanelDataSummary, Input: frames: DataFrame[] = [], Output: PanelDataSummary. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must create a standalone function named getPanelDataSummary matching the interface specification.

title: 1.2: Exports the getPanelDataSummary function and PanelDataSummary type from the @grafana/data public API

annotations.description: The getPanelDataSummary function and PanelDataSummary type are exported from the @grafana/data package entry point so external consumers can import them.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 29 shows: export { type PanelDataSummary, getPanelDataSummary } from './panel/suggestions/getPanelDataSummary'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The function and its interface should be part of the @grafana/data public API.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The @grafana/data package exports public utilities from packages/grafana-data/src/index.ts. [3: Logical Necessity] For external consumers (such as PanelDataErrorView) to import getPanelDataSummary, it must be exported from the package entry point. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must export both the function and type from @grafana/data.

title: 1.3: Adds a fieldCountByType method to PanelDataSummary that returns the count of fields for a given FieldType

annotations.description: The PanelDataSummary interface includes a fieldCountByType method that accepts a FieldType parameter and returns a number representing how many fields of that type exist across all frames.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 59 shows: fieldCountByType: (type: FieldType) => number in the PanelDataSummary interface.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The field type counting should also be generalized so you can query the count... of any field type without needing a dedicated property for each one.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface specifies: PanelDataSummary provides fieldCountByType(type: FieldType) returning number. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must add a fieldCountByType method to PanelDataSummary.

title: 1.4: Adds a hasFieldType method to PanelDataSummary that returns whether any field of a given FieldType exists

annotations.description: The PanelDataSummary interface includes a hasFieldType method that accepts a FieldType parameter and returns a boolean indicating whether at least one field of that type exists across all frames.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 60 shows: hasFieldType: (type: FieldType) => boolean in the PanelDataSummary interface.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The field type counting should also be generalized so you can query the count or presence of any field type without needing a dedicated property for each one.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The interface specifies: PanelDataSummary provides hasFieldType(type: FieldType) returning boolean. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must add a hasFieldType method to PanelDataSummary.

title: 1.5: Updates VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder to delegate summary computation to getPanelDataSummary

annotations.description: The VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder class in packages/grafana-data/src/types/panel.ts delegates its dataSummary computation to the extracted getPanelDataSummary function rather than computing it internally.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 182 shows: this.dataSummary = getPanelDataSummary(this.data?.series) replacing this.computeDataSummary() in the constructor.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Can you refactor the data summary into a standalone utility that can be used independently of the builder?' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder in packages/grafana-data/src/types/panel.ts currently computes dataSummary via a private method computeDataSummary(). [3: Logical Necessity] After extraction, this class must delegate to the new function to avoid duplicating the logic. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must update VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder to call the standalone function.

title: 1.6: Updates consumers (such as PanelDataErrorView) that instantiate VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder solely for dataSummary to call getPanelDataSummary directly

annotations.description: Code that previously created a VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder only to access its dataSummary property is updated to call getPanelDataSummary directly with the data series.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 269-271 show PanelDataErrorView.tsx changed from 'const builder = new VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder(props.data); const { dataSummary } = builder' to 'const dataSummary = getPanelDataSummary(props.data.series)'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Any code that currently instantiates a builder just to get the summary should use the new utility directly instead.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] PanelDataErrorView in public/app/features/panel/components/PanelDataErrorView.tsx instantiates VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder solely to read dataSummary. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must update such consumers to call the utility directly.

title: 1.7: Marks all 6 existing per-type properties (numberFieldCount, timeFieldCount, stringFieldCount, hasNumberField, hasTimeField, hasStringField) on PanelDataSummary as deprecated (such as via @deprecated JSDoc annotations)

annotations.description: All 6 existing hardcoded per-type properties (numberFieldCount, timeFieldCount, stringFieldCount, hasNumberField, hasTimeField, hasStringField) are marked as deprecated on the PanelDataSummary interface, indicating that consumers should use the new generic methods instead.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 65-76 show @deprecated JSDoc annotations on each per-type property, e.g. '@deprecated use PanelDataSummary.fieldCountByType(FieldType.number)'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The existing per-type properties should be preserved for backward compatibility but marked as deprecated.' [3: Logical Necessity] Deprecation markers guide consumers to migrate to the new generic methods. Without them, consumers have no signal that the old API is superseded. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must mark the per-type properties as deprecated using an appropriate mechanism.

title: 3.1: Removes the embedded computeDataSummary method from VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder in packages/grafana-data/src/types/panel.ts

annotations.description: The private computeDataSummary() method or equivalent inline logic is removed from VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder since the class now delegates to getPanelDataSummary.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 189-242 show removal of the private computeDataSummary() method from VisualizationSuggestionsBuilder.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After delegating to getPanelDataSummary, the private computeDataSummary() method becomes dead code. [4: Qualitative Requirement] Good refactoring removes redundant code to avoid confusion and maintenance burden. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer should remove the embedded computation logic.

title: 4.1: Removes existing PanelDataSummary per-type properties (such as numberFieldCount, hasNumberField) without providing them for backward compatibility

annotations.description: Detects if the refactoring breaks existing consumers by completely removing properties like numberFieldCount, timeFieldCount, stringFieldCount, hasNumberField, hasTimeField, or hasStringField from PanelDataSummary without preserving them.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 65-76 show the deprecated fields are preserved on the interface with @deprecated annotations, and lines 121-126 show the function still populates them.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The existing per-type properties should be preserved for backward compatibility but marked as deprecated.' [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] Existing consumer code throughout the codebase references these property names directly. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT remove these properties from the PanelDataSummary return value.

title: 4.2: Introduces compilation errors in modified TypeScript files

annotations.description: Detects if the refactoring introduces code that would fail to compile due to syntax errors, missing imports, type mismatches, or undefined references in any modified file.
annotations.evidence: All files modified by the solution must remain valid TypeScript that compiles without errors.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] Compilation errors would make the refactoring unusable and break the build. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT introduce compilation errors in any modified file.

title: 4.3: Modifies test files despite the explicit constraint not to do so

annotations.description: Detects if the agent modifies, adds, or removes any test files (such as files matching *.test.ts, *.test.tsx, *.spec.ts, or files in test/ directories) despite the prompt explicitly stating not to.
annotations.evidence: The prompt ends with: 'I've already taken care of all changes to the test files. Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way. Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.' [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT modify any test files.
